statement.markdown: Given a trie, implement a function to insert a string into the trie. However, there is a bug where it incorrectly handles duplicate string values. Fix this bug to ensure that duplicate values are handled correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input is a string of lowercase English letters.
statement.outputFormat: If the string is inserted into the trie, return True, otherwise return False.
statement.notes: The trie node value is the first character of the string.

examples.explanation: apple is a new string that should be inserted into the trie.
ple is a duplicate of the existing string "apple" in the trie, so it should not be inserted.

hints.text: Check if the current node's value is the same as the first character of the string

editorial.title: Fixing Trie Node Value Duplication
editorial.intuition: The goal is to check if the current node's value is the same as the first character of the string, and if so, return False.
editorial.bruteForce: Checking all nodes in the trie for duplicates can be time-consuming.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a boolean flag to track whether the current node's value is the same as the first character of the string.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a boolean flag to track whether the current node's value is the same as the first character of the string, and if so, return False. Otherwise, continue traversing the trie.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected function ensures that duplicate string values are handled correctly by returning False when the current node's value is the same as the first character of the string.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check for duplicate values
Not handling the base case correctly
